homepage.heroHeadline: Lawn care tailored to Raleigh's climate
homepage.heroSubline: We understand how Piedmont clay and humid summers affect your lawn. Our team designs maintenance plans that work with Raleigh's unique conditions.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex Lawn Care began when our founder noticed how many Raleigh homeowners struggled with drainage issues in the region's red clay soil. What started as a one-truck operation now serves hundreds of homes across Wake County.

We take a scientific approach to lawn health, testing soil composition and selecting grass varieties that thrive in our climate. Our methods reduce water waste and prevent the fungal growth that plagues humid summers.
As Raleigh grows, we're committed to preserving green spaces. We partner with local schools for turf education programs and donate services to maintain Pullen Park's historic landscapes.

valueProps.title: Clay soil specialists
valueProps.description: Our aeration techniques combat compaction in Raleigh's dense red clay, improving root growth and drainage.

valueProps.title: Climate-appropriate care
valueProps.description: We adjust fertilization schedules for Raleigh's long growing season and sudden spring storms.

valueProps.title: Neighborhood knowledge
valueProps.description: From ITB's mature oaks to North Hills' new construction, we understand each area's landscaping needs.

valueProps.title: Tech-enabled service
valueProps.description: Real-time scheduling and photo updates keep you informed without disrupting your Research Triangle workday.

homepage.ctaSubline: Schedule your free assessment today. We'll analyze your soil and create a customized plan.

services.name: Core Aeration
services.shortDescription: Essential treatment for Raleigh's compacted clay soils
services.description:
Piedmont red clay becomes concrete-hard by mid-summer, starving grass roots of air and water. Our heavy-duty aerators remove 3-inch plugs to break up compaction without damaging delicate topsoil.

We time treatments for early spring and fall when soil moisture is ideal. Post-service, we recommend overseeding with drought-resistant fescue blends that perform well in our climate. For properties near NC State's older neighborhoods, we take extra care around mature tree roots.

Most Raleigh lawns need annual aeration, but newer developments with imported topsoil may require less frequent service. We'll assess your specific conditions during our consultation.
process: Step 1: Mark irrigation heads and shallow utilities
Step 2: Double-pass aeration pattern for complete coverage
Step 3: Remove and compost soil plugs
Step 4: Apply soil conditioner to accelerate recovery
benefits: Reduces water runoff by 40% in clay soils
Allows fertilizers to reach root zones
Prevents thatch buildup common in Raleigh's humid climate
Improves resilience against summer heat stress

faq.question: Will aeration damage my sprinkler system?
faq.answer: We use utility maps and physical markers to avoid irrigation lines. In 12 years, we've never caused sprinkler damage.

faq.question: How soon can I mow after aeration?
faq.answer: Wait 48 hours to allow soil structure to stabilize. The small holes will naturally fill in within 2-3 weeks.

services.name: Drought-Resistant Overseeding
services.shortDescription: Turf renewal for Raleigh's hot summers
services.description:
Traditional Kentucky bluegrass often fails in our climate, leaving thin patches by August. We use tall fescue blends specifically bred for the Southeast's heat and humidity.

Our process begins with dethatching to remove the spongy layer that harbors fungi. We then slice-seed at two angles for maximum germination, using a biodegradable mulch to retain moisture during establishment. For downtown properties with limited sun, we incorporate fine fescues that tolerate shade.

The best time for overseeding in Raleigh is late September through October, when cooler nights aid growth but soil remains warm. We adjust seed rates based on your lawn's sun exposure and foot traffic patterns.
process: Step 1: Scalp existing grass to 1.5 inches
Step 2: Power rake to remove thatch
Step 3: Apply pre-emergent herbicide-free seed starter
Step 4: Slice-seed with 50% overlap pattern
benefits: Extends green season by 6-8 weeks
Reduces water needs by 30% compared to traditional turf
Creates dense growth that crowds out crabgrass
Self-repairs from dog spots and high-traffic areas

faq.question: Will the new grass look different?
faq.answer: We match cultivars to your existing turf for seamless blending. The improved varieties maintain color better during drought.

faq.question: How long until we can walk on the lawn?
faq.answer: Limit foot traffic for 3 weeks. The grass will be established enough for normal use after 4-6 weeks.

services.name: Storm Recovery
services.shortDescription: Emergency cleanup after Raleigh's severe weather
services.description:
Spring thunderstorms and occasional winter ice leave lawns littered with debris. Our rapid-response teams prioritize safety hazards like hanging limbs before addressing cosmetic damage.

For flooded areas common in low-lying Cary neighborhoods, we use specialized vacuums to remove silt without tearing turf. When trees fall on lawns, we repair ruts with our proprietary soil mix that mimics Piedmont clay composition.

We keep emergency slots open from March-June for peak storm season. Same-day service is available for elderly residents and households with small children. All debris is recycled through Raleigh's yard waste program.
process: Step 1: Safety assessment of the property
Step 2: Priority removal of hazardous debris
Step 3: Soil grading and turf repair
Step 4: Apply fungicide to prevent waterborne diseases
benefits: Prevents mold growth in damp debris piles
Reduces mosquito breeding sites after flooding
Saves damaged grass with prompt care
Works directly with insurance for tree damage claims

faq.question: Do you handle large tree removals?
faq.answer: We partner with licensed arborists for trunks over 12 inches in diameter. Our focus is lawn restoration after the tree is removed.

faq.question: Will you bill my insurance directly?
faq.answer: We provide documentation for claims but require payment upfront. Most policies reimburse within 30 days.

services.name: Irrigation Winterization
services.shortDescription: Protect sprinkler systems from Raleigh's freeze-thaw cycles
services.description:
While our winters are mild, sudden temperature drops can burst pipes in un-winterized systems. We use industrial air compressors to blow out all water, focusing on vulnerable backflow preventers common in Morrisville's newer installations.

The ideal time for winterization is mid-October through November, after the last mowing but before consistent freezing begins. We tag each zone as it's completed and provide digital documentation of the service.

For historic homes Inside the Beltline with older galvanized pipes, we recommend additional insulation. Our technicians are trained to identify early corrosion that could lead to spring leaks.
process: Step 1: Shut off and drain main water supply
Step 2: Connect compressor to blow out each zone
Step 3: Manual drain of low-point valves
Step 4: Insulate above-ground components
benefits: Prevents costly pipe repairs come spring
Identifies worn valves needing replacement
Extends system lifespan by 3-5 years
Includes free spring startup inspection

faq.question: Do I need this if we rarely freeze?
faq.answer: Yes. Even one night below 25°F can damage systems. The 2022 Christmas freeze caused $200,000+ in local irrigation repairs.

faq.question: Can I winterize my own system?
faq.answer: DIY attempts often leave water in lateral lines. Our 100 CFM compressors remove 98% more moisture than consumer models.

services.name: Organic Fertilization
services.shortDescription: Slow-release nutrients for Raleigh's growing season
services.description:
Chemical fertilizers wash through our clay soils during heavy rains, ending up in Jordan Lake. Our organic blends feed lawns gradually while improving soil structure.

We use poultry-based nitrogen from North Carolina farms, combined with mycorrhizal fungi that help roots access nutrients. Applications are timed with Raleigh's growth patterns: early spring for greening, late spring for disease resistance, and fall for winter hardiness.

For homes near Lake Raleigh or other watersheds, we offer phosphorus-free formulas. All products are safe for pets and children once watered in, typically within 2 hours of application.
process: Step 1: Soil test to identify deficiencies
Step 2: Custom blend formulation
Step 3: Calibrated spreader application
Step 4: Light watering to activate microbes
benefits: Builds drought tolerance over time
Won't burn lawn during summer heat
Supports local agriculture
Reduces algal blooms in area lakes

faq.question: Why does organic fertilizer look like chicken feed?
faq.answer: It contains actual poultry meal - a sustainable nitrogen source that's processed at lower temperatures than synthetic options.

faq.question: How soon will I see results?
faq.answer: Initial greening appears in 10-14 days versus 48 hours with chemicals, but effects last 50% longer.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: After years of watching water pool in my Five Points backyard, Apex Lawn Care's aeration made an immediate difference. They explained how the red clay was preventing drainage and even showed me the soil plugs. My lawn finally absorbs rain instead of shedding it onto the sidewalk.

reviews.name: Nina R.
reviews.text: The fescue blend they used in our North Hills yard survived last July's heatwave when every other lawn on the street went brown. I appreciate how they matched the new grass to our existing turf - you can't tell where they reseeded except it's noticeably thicker.

reviews.name: David and Sarah K.
reviews.text: When an oak limb crushed our garden in Brier Creek, their team arrived within hours. They carefully removed debris without damaging our perennials, then leveled the ruts with soil that matched our existing grade. The repair was so seamless our neighbors didn't realize we'd had damage.

reviews.name: Elena P.
reviews.text: As NC State employees, we're conscious of runoff into Lake Raleigh. Their organic program has given us the greenest lawn on the block without the guilt. The soil test revealed our phosphorus levels were high, so they customized a blend that's helping balance our soil naturally.

reviews.name: James L.
reviews.text: They caught a cracked backflow preventer during winterization that would've flooded our Morrisville foundation come spring. The technician took time to show me the issue and explain how the winter freeze-thaw cycles cause metal fatigue. Preventative maintenance pays off.

reviews.name: The Henderson Family
reviews.text: From spring aeration through fall leaf cleanup, Apex has kept our historic ITB lawn looking pristine. They understand how shade from our 1950s oaks affects grass growth and adjust their approach accordingly. After bad experiences with national chains, we value their local expertise.

service-areas.title: Lawn Care Service Areas | Raleigh-Durham Region
service-areas.description: We provide specialized lawn services throughout Wake County including Durham, Cary, Chapel Hill, Apex, and Morrisville neighborhoods.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Lawn Care proudly serves Durham, NC, a vibrant city with a mix of historic charm and modern revitalization. Our team specializes in maintaining lush lawns in Durham's renovated industrial lofts and residential neighborhoods, ensuring your outdoor space thrives despite the hot, humid summers. From routine mowing to seasonal fertilization, we tailor our services to Durham's unique climate and soil conditions.
serviceAreas.localContext: Durham blends historic tobacco warehouses with trendy downtown lofts and a thriving arts scene.

serviceAreas.content: In Cary, NC, Apex Lawn Care helps homeowners maintain pristine lawns in the city's master-planned communities. With neighborhoods primarily built from the 1980s to 2000s, Cary's landscapes require consistent care to combat the hot, humid summers. Our services include weed control, aeration, and irrigation management to keep Cary's lawns healthy year-round.
serviceAreas.localContext: Cary is known for its well-planned subdivisions, top-rated schools, and family-friendly atmosphere.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Lawn Care serves Chapel Hill, NC, home to the University of North Carolina and diverse housing styles. From student rentals to faculty homes, we provide customized lawn care solutions for Chapel Hill's varied landscapes. Our team understands the challenges of hot, humid summers and offers shade-tolerant grass treatments for tree-lined properties.
serviceAreas.localContext: Chapel Hill combines collegiate energy with historic neighborhoods and a strong sense of community.

serviceAreas.content: As a hometown business, Apex Lawn Care takes special pride in serving Apex, NC's newer family-oriented developments. Built primarily from the 2000s to 2020s, these neighborhoods benefit from our modern lawn care techniques. We address common issues in Apex's hot summers, including drought stress and pest management, while keeping your lawn safe for kids and pets.
serviceAreas.localContext: Apex features planned communities with amenities like pools, parks, and walking trails.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Lawn Care serves Morrisville, NC, where tech workers and Research Triangle Park employees take pride in their homes. We specialize in maintaining the manicured lawns common in Morrisville's newer housing developments. Our services combat the challenges of hot, humid summers while respecting HOA guidelines common in the area.
serviceAreas.localContext: Morrisville offers convenient access to RTP with a mix of apartments and single-family homes.

serviceAreas.content: In Holly Springs, NC, Apex Lawn Care helps residents maintain beautiful lawns in this fast-growing suburb. With many homes built in the 2000s and 2010s, we provide specialized care for young landscapes establishing their root systems. Our summer lawn programs address heat stress while keeping your outdoor space vibrant for backyard gatherings.
serviceAreas.localContext: Holly Springs features newer subdivisions with community pools and athletic facilities.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Lawn Care serves Wake Forest, NC, where historic charm meets modern development. We help maintain lawns in both the town's older neighborhoods and newer subdivisions. Our team understands the specific needs of Wake Forest properties, from irrigation system maintenance to dealing with summer humidity's effects on grass health.
serviceAreas.localContext: Wake Forest combines a quaint downtown with rapid residential growth and excellent schools.

serviceAreas.content: In Fuquay-Varina, NC, Apex Lawn Care provides comprehensive lawn services for this growing town. We cater to both the historic district's unique landscapes and newer developments' manicured lawns. Our summer care programs help Fuquay-Varina lawns withstand heat waves while maintaining their curb appeal.
serviceAreas.localContext: Fuquay-Varina blends small-town character with rapid growth and affordable housing options.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Lawn Care serves Garner, NC, helping homeowners maintain healthy lawns in this affordable Raleigh suburb. We understand Garner's mix of established neighborhoods and new construction, offering tailored services for each. Our summer lawn treatments combat the stress of hot, humid weather while promoting deep root growth.
serviceAreas.localContext: Garner offers more affordable housing options while being minutes from downtown Raleigh.

serviceAreas.content: In Knightdale, NC, Apex Lawn Care provides expert lawn maintenance for this eastern Wake County community. We help Knightdale homeowners keep their lawns lush despite the challenging summer heat and humidity. From new construction sites to established properties, we offer customized care plans for all lawn types.
serviceAreas.localContext: Knightdale features a mix of rural areas and newer subdivisions with easy highway access.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Lawn Care serves Rolesville, NC, one of the fastest-growing towns in Wake County. We specialize in maintaining lawns in Rolesville's newer subdivisions, where young grass needs careful attention during hot summers. Our services help establish healthy turf that can withstand North Carolina's humid climate.
serviceAreas.localContext: Rolesville offers spacious lots and newer construction with small-town charm.

serviceAreas.content: In Clayton, NC, Apex Lawn Care helps homeowners maintain beautiful lawns in this Johnston County community. We understand Clayton's mix of rural properties and newer subdivisions, offering appropriate care for each. Our summer lawn programs address the challenges of heat and humidity while keeping your property looking its best.
serviceAreas.localContext: Clayton combines small-town affordability with convenient access to Raleigh.
